steps.title: Identify what the passage actually says
steps.content:
The team tested three Paleolithic light sources — torches, animal-fat lamps, and fireplaces — and determined each "likely had a specific purpose." The concrete example given is that animal-fat lamps were less useful than torches while walking because they did not illuminate the cave floor. The passage is about testing methods and drawing conclusions about how artists moved through dark caves.
  • Choice A — 'fireplaces were essential to the creators of Paleolithic cave art': The passage never claims fireplaces were "essential" — it only says each source had a specific purpose. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Paleolithic cave artists used animal-fat lamps more often than torches': The passage says lamps were less useful than torches for walking, not that they were used more often. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the team was reluctant to draw many conclusions': The passage shows the team drawing confident, specific conclusions — no reluctance appears. ✗
  • Choice D — 'tested Paleolithic light sources and learned details about how artists traveled within dark caves': Matches both the method (testing) and the outcome (understanding navigation). ✓

steps.title: Confirm the main idea match
steps.content: The passage is structured around a research method (testing replicas of three light sources) and its result (learning how each source functioned during movement in dark caves). Choice D captures both elements precisely while the other choices either overstate, contradict, or misread the tone of the passage. That matches choice D — D is correct.
